headline: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
summary: Author Dave Eggers addressed OpenAI staff last year. He reportedly stated that ChatGPT is "silencing an entire generation." Eggers, a prolific writer and arts advocate, shared strong concerns about AI's impact.

headline: Google might not kneecap the Pixel 11a with an old processor
summary: Leaks suggest the Pixel 11a might feature a flagship Tensor G6 processor. This would be an upgrade from the Pixel 10a, which used the older Tensor G4. The Pixel 'a' series typically used modern chips, making the 10a's G4 a disappointment.

headline: Google is open-sourcing its 3D emoji
summary: Google is making its 3D emoji designs available for public use. This move was announced on World Emoji Day, including design insights. Users can now incorporate Google's 3D emoji into their own creative projects.

headline: GoPro’s discounted Max 2 bundle includes $100 worth of accessories
summary: The GoPro Max 2 accessory bundle is currently discounted to $369. This deal includes $100 worth of extra accessories. A 360-degree camera is ideal for capturing all action, making this a notable sale.

headline: Matt Damon Wants New Jason Bourne Movie to 'Be Like the First 3' as Work With Conclave Director Continues
summary: Matt Damon provided an update on the new Jason Bourne movie. He hopes the new film will recapture the essence of the original trilogy. Damon is collaborating with director Edward Berger on the project.

headline: The Best Deals Today: PNY RTX 5070 OC, Apple AirPods 4, The Dark Knight Trilogy, and More
summary: IGN is highlighting today's top tech and entertainment deals. Sales include the PNY RTX 5070 OC graphics card and Apple AirPods 4. The Dark Knight Trilogy and other products are also featured in the deals list.

headline: Steam Machine Engineers at Valve Signal Grim Future as Memory Crisis Is 'Still Getting Worse'
summary: Valve's Steam Machine engineers warn about the worsening memory crisis. The ongoing tech issue is driving up prices for consumers. They indicate a grim outlook for buyers in the current market.

headline: Call of Duty Movie to Take Place in the Modern Warfare Universe, Director Confirms
summary: The upcoming Call of Duty movie will be set in the Modern Warfare universe. Paramount Pictures and Activision confirmed the universe choice. The film aims to adapt the popular game series' storyline.

headline: Andrew Tate and brother arrested in US after more charges laid against them in UK
summary: Andrew Tate and his brother have been arrested in the US. Their arrest follows new charges filed against them in the UK. The Crown Prosecution Service is seeking their extradition from the US.

headline: Andy Burnham to scrap digital ID to focus on cost of living in first major policy pledge
summary: Incoming PM Andy Burnham plans to scrap the digital ID initiative. His government will prioritize addressing the cost of living crisis. This marks Burnham's first significant policy pledge.

headline: Can Burnham's casual style survive contact with No 10?
summary: The article questions if Andy Burnham's casual style can endure as PM. His "wardrobe" is central to his political brand. Becoming Prime Minister may necessitate a change in his public image.

headline: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
summary: An Iranian attack in Jordan resulted in two US troops killed. One US service member is currently reported missing. Hostilities between Iran and the US have re-escalated with recent strikes.
